originalTitle: Morning Mail: BHP’s climate fail revealed, pope denounces AI, life on the world’s longest golf course
summary: In a Guardian exclusive, leaked documents show the miner has hit the brakes on decarbonisation plans
content: Morning everyone. We reveal leaked documents showing the world’s biggest mining company has hit the brakes on decarbonising its vast Pilbara operations in Western Australia. Experts say the massive polluter dragging its feet on climate action could have national – and global – implications. The pope has denounced AI, we have a podcast with David Pocock about why he isn’t ruling out a “teal” political party, and we check out the Nullarbor Links, the world’s longest golf course at 1,365km. Australia Bird count | The Australian magpie has again topped the charts in BirdLife Australia’s Aussie Bird Count as the feathered friend most often spotted across the country. Budget push | Anthony Albanese will present controversial changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax to parliament on Thursday, pushing for speedy passage of the plans while flagging possible carve-outs for businesses beyond the startup sector. Flotilla claims | Australians returning home from detention in Israel say they were abused, tortured and demeaned while in custody and have asked to meet the prime minister, Anthony Albanese, to discuss their treatment. ‘Homesickness’ | A damning NSW auditor general’s report has found that the $100m relief scheme has not delivered a single home or lot since the devastating 2022 Lismore floods. Far for the course | Stretching 1,365km across the remote Australian outback, the Nullarbor Links takes a week to complete and inspires devotion among the players, whose annual competition has just finished. World Tehran doubts | Iran has poured cold water on suggestions that a peace deal with the US is imminent, pointing to the confusion in US positions and Israeli interference as key factors in why a complete agreement is proving difficult to secure. Meanwhile, Israel intensified strikes on Lebanon. Earlier, oil prices fell below $100 a barrel on hopes of a deal. Ebola warning | The World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak is outpacing response efforts and countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are at high risk from the disease. Papal bull | Pope Leo has denounced the “culture of power” driving the rapid rise of artificial intelligence while warning that the technology must be subject to the “most rigorous” ethical constraints as it infiltrates everything from work to war. Cave rescue | Divers who helped in the dramatic rescue of a young Thai football team in 2018 have joined efforts to free seven people who have been trapped for five days inside a remote, flooded cave in central Laos. Cheese roll | Tom Kopke, a YouTuber from Germany, has out-tumbled local hero Chris Anderson in the annual cheese-rolling contest in a sweltering Gloucestershire. Full Story David Pocock on whether a ‘teal’ party is possible Independent senator David Pocock tells Reged Ahmad he is open to the idea of a teal “party” but that it is not without risks. Our chief political correspondent, Dan Jervis-Bardy, has explained what is driving the debate. In-depth An exclusive investigation based on documents leaked to Guardian Australia and the ABC show that BHP has delayed major climate projects, made new purchases of polluting diesel trucks and scrapped a proposed plant that would have cut vast amounts of emissions. The revelations contained in a leaked internal memo raise serious questions about the Albanese government’s climate policies and whether they allow companies such as BHP to delay action without penalty. It shows how the company scrapped a plan to build greener facilities in the Pilbara, and our environment editor argues the miner must act on emissions. Not the news In shades of the hit medical TV series The Pitt, Roland Bull talks us through his first experience as a surgical assistant in the latest edition of our series on the jobs that changed people’s lives. Sport Tennis | Alex de Minaur eased past British qualifier Toby Samuel in straight sets at Roland Garros, while a rejuvenated Daria Kasatkina powered through to the second round and Thanasi Kokkinakis won another trademark five-set comeback. Swimming | Cam McEvoy has had a dig at the inaugural Enhanced Games with a post saying “Seriously?! That’s all you got!” after Greece’s Kristian Gkolomeev went under McEvoy’s 50m freestyle world record time at the Games in Las Vegas. NRL | Andrew Abdo left the NRL’s top job yesterday but his exit emerged as something of a punchline for Peter V’landys, the wisecracking chair who now takes full control of the sport. Media roundup The Sydney Morning Herald reports Australia will spend nearly $150m on Chris Bowen’s role as president of negotiations at the UN’s next climate conference. One Nation’s lead over the Coalition has reached its widest margin since the Liberal leadership spill that forced Sussan Ley out, a Telegraph poll reports. Jacinta Allan has rejected the prime minister’s gun buyback scheme, according to the Age. One in four taxi passengers have been ripped off by dodgy drivers and “busted meters”, the Courier Mail claims. What’s happening today Technology | Commonwealth Bank AI conference in Sydney with speakers including the bank’s chief executive, Matt Comyn, Open AI boss Sam Altman and Coles boss Leah Weckert. Canberra | Former treasurer Joe Hockey and a former Trump acting chief of staff, Mick Mulvaney, speak at the National Press Club. Sign up Sign up for the Breaking News Australia email If you would like to receive this Morning Mail update to your email inbox every weekday, sign up here, or finish your day with our Afternoon Update newsletter. You can follow the latest in US politics by signing up for This Week in Trumpland. Brain teaser And finally, here are the Guardian’s crosswords to keep you entertained throughout the day. Until tomorrow. Quick crossword Cryptic crossword Contact us If you have a story tip or technical issue viewing this newsletter, please reply to this email. chineseTitle: 早间邮报：必和必拓在气候方面的失败被揭露，教皇谴责 AI，世界上最长的高尔夫球场上的生活
chineseSummary: 在《卫报》独家报道中，泄露的文件显示该矿商已停止脱碳计划
chineseContent: 大家早上好。我们披露的泄露文件显示，全球最大的矿业公司已停止其位于西澳大利亚的皮尔巴拉大型业务的脱碳工作。专家表示，这个巨大的污染者在气候行动上拖延可能会产生国家乃至全球的影响。教皇谴责了人工智能，我们与 David Pocock 进行了播客，探讨了他为何不排除“青色”政党的可能性，我们还参观了纳拉伯林克斯 (Nullarbor Links)，这是世界上最长的高尔夫球场，全长 1,365 公里。澳大利亚鸟类统计 |澳大利亚喜鹊再次在澳大利亚鸟类协会的澳大利亚鸟类统计中名列前茅，成为全国最常见的有羽毛的朋友。预算推动|安东尼·阿尔博内塞(Anthony Albanese)将于周四向议会提出有关负扣税和资本利得税的有争议的改革，推动这些计划的迅速通过，同时指出可能将初创企业以外的企业排除在外。船队索赔 |从以色列拘留所返回家园的澳大利亚人表示，他们在拘留期间受到虐待、酷刑和侮辱，并要求与总理安东尼·阿尔巴内塞会面，讨论他们的待遇。 ‘思乡’|新南威尔士州审计长的一份令人震惊的报告发现，自 2022 年毁灭性的利斯莫尔洪水以来，1 亿澳元的救援计划尚未交付任何房屋或土地。距离课程还很远|纳拉伯林克斯赛道全长 1,365 公里，横跨澳大利亚偏远内陆地区，需要一周的时间才能完成，一年一度的比赛刚刚结束，它激发了球员们的热情。世界德黑兰的疑虑|伊朗对即将与美国达成和平协议的说法泼了冷水，指出美国立场的混乱和以色列的干涉是难以达成全面协议的关键因素。与此同时，以色列打算
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
